Jul – Aug 2026

A gap nobody
is filling.
Not formal. Not casual.
Just wearable.

Most everyday Indian clothing is either ethnic or generic. Neither feels right. Fast fashion feels wrong. Sustainable brands feel too plain or too expensive.

Sneh & Co exists in between. Clean, modern, rooted in natural fabric. Designed for the version of you that goes from a laptop to a meeting to dinner — without changing. Without explaining yourself.

Sneh
In Sanskrit — love, warmth. The quiet feeling of something that is exactly right.
Sneh
In textile language — the oil that softens. The thing that makes fabric feel like it belongs on your skin.
Both meanings,
deliberately chosen.

The fabric is not
incidental.
It is the product.

SnehWeave — 50% banana fiber, 50% cotton — is the material every Sneh & Co piece is built from. Banana fiber comes from agricultural waste: stems left after harvest that would otherwise be burned. It breathes. It softens with use. It earns its place not for the story, but for what it actually feels like in Indian weather.

50%
Banana fiber

From waste stems. Breathable, moisture-wicking. Gets softer with every wash.

50%
Cotton

Holds shape. Drapes well. Familiar to wear. Doesn't crease badly in Indian weather.

155
GSM weight

Light enough for Indian summers. Substantial enough to hang and drape like a real garment.

Why Sneh & Co exists

"Most clothes are made for one version of your body.
We design for all the versions."

Not ethnic, not generic. Not fast fashion, not a lecture about sustainability. Just well-made, natural-fabric clothing for modern Indian life — at a price that doesn't require a justification conversation with yourself.

Be first.
Not almost first.

Opening to a small group before public launch. Waitlist members get first access, first fits, and the launch price locked in.
